title: rangium ovatum
intro: Rangium ovatum, also known as the Desert Marigold or Bush Sunflower, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It contains bioactive compounds with medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, viruses, and fungi. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Rangium ovatum is often made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils to take advantage of its health benefits.
